title: 10 best herbal creams for sore eyes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with sore eyes because of their unique blend of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as calendula oil, work to reduce redness and swelling in the affected area, providing relief from discomfort and itchiness. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to soothe dry or irritated eyes, promoting a healthy and comfortable visual experience. This natural remedy is especially beneficial for individuals who spend extended periods in front of screens or are prone to eye strain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sore eyes.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants in pot marigold cream protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to age-related macular degeneration and other eye problems.
Soothing and moisturizing: Pot marigold's emollient properties help soothe dry, irritated eyes and provide long-lasting moisture, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy eye function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks of the pot marigold plant are rich in tannins, which provide astringent and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness and swelling in sore eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 50g of calendula officinalis flowers in a warm oven for 24 hours.
Combine the dried flowers with 200ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler at medium heat.
Simmer the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl.
Mix the infused oil with 100g of beeswax and 20g of vitamin e oil until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with sore eyes because of its natural soothing properties that calm irritated eyelids and reduce inflammation. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which work to alleviate redness and discomfort caused by dryness, allergies, or computer use. Additionally, aloe's moisturizing effects help to hydrate the eyes, reducing itchiness and stiffness, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing sore and tired eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from dry, irritated, or inflamed eyes.
Soften and lubricate property: Aloe vera's glycoproteins and amino acids help moisturize and soften the eye tissue, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by dry eyes or allergies.

Antibacterial property: The antimicrobial compounds in aloe vera, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause conjunctivitis, pink eye, and other infections, promoting healing and preventing further infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their gel-like interior, aloe leaves are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Roots: Aloe roots contain a unique combination of flavonoids and anthraquinones that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in eye irritations.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality aloe vera gel and other natural ingredients such as coconut oil and beeswax in sufficient quantities for the recipe.
Combine one-quarter cup of aloe vera gel with three tablespoons of coconut oil in a small mixing bowl and stir well to blend.
Melt two teaspoons of beeswax in a double boiler or microwave-safe bowl and gently pour it into the aloe vera mixture stirring constantly to combine.
Continue heating the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 120°f to 140°f, then remove from heat and let cool slightly for ten minutes.
Pour the cream into an air-tight container and refrigerate for at least one hour before applying to help soothe sore eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with sore eyes because they contain the anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea, which has been traditionally used to soothe irritated eyes. The cream's hydrating ingredients also help to calm and moisturize dry, itchy eyes, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, its antioxidant properties may help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall eye health and reducing the appearance of puffiness and dark circles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas coneflower creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, which help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in sore eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy vision and reducing inflammation.
Sothoic compounds: Kansas coneflower creams contain soothing compounds like saponins and terpenes that help calm irritated eyes, reduce redness, and promote a sense of comfort and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, Kansas coneflower roots have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated eye tissues.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of Kansas coneflower contain a high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which are known for their potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, beneficial for reducing redness and inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground echinacea powder with 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Stir in 10g of beeswax and 5g of shea butter until fully incorporated and melted, then remove from heat.
Allow the mixture to cool slightly before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 2-3 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with sore eyes because they harness the natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of the plantain herb. The cream's active ingredients, such as allantoin and mucilage, work together to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dry or irritated eyes. By providing a protective barrier on the surface of the eye, the cream also helps to retain moisture and promote healing, offering fast and effective relief from sore eyes caused by a variety of factors, including allergies, dry air, and computer use.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and redness in the eye, providing relief from irritation and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in plantain cream neutralize free radicals and protect the delicate tissues of the eye from damage caused by oxidative stress, which can contribute to sore eyes.

Soothing and protective mucilage properties: Plantain cream's thick, gel-like mucilage helps soothe and shield the eye surface, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with dryness, itchiness, or scratchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are used to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: With their antioxidant properties, barks help protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Chop the washed leaves into small pieces and soak them in hot distilled water at a ratio of 1:4 for 30 minutes.
Transfer the soaked leaf mixture to a blender or food processor and blend until smooth with no visible particles remaining.
Strain the blended leaf mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids in 10 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the strained leaf liquid with 80 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil and store the cream at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright creams helps with sore eyes because they contain soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that directly address the discomfort. The herbal ingredients, such as chamomile and calendula, calm irritated tissues and reduce redness. Additionally, eyebright's natural astringent qualities help to constrict blood vessels, reducing puffiness and swelling. As a result, eyebright creams provide fast and effective relief from dry, itchy, or burning eyes, making them an ideal solution for those seeking a natural remedy for sore eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Eyebright creams contain antioxidants such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the eyes, thus soothing sore eyes.

Wound-healing agents: Herbal extracts like chamomile and calendula have been shown to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue oxygenation, and reducing scar formation, which can aid in the recovery of dry or irritated eyes.

Astringent and anti-inflammatory flavonoids: Flavonoids present in eyebright creams, such as quercetin and luteolin, have astringent properties that help reduce puffiness and inflammation, while also providing antioxidant protection against oxidative stress, which can contribute to sore eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and inflammation.
Leaves: Containing essential oils and flavonoids, leaves have anti-inflammatory properties that ease eye strain and promote healthy vision.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers, washing them gently in cold water to remove dirt.
Chop the plant material into small pieces, measuring out 20 grams of leaf and 10 grams of flower per 100 milliliters of menstruum.
Combine the chopped plant material with 100 milliliters of 50% ethanol and allow it to macerate for 2 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the macerated mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Mix the resulting tincture with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil, heating the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with sore eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream contains apigenin, an antioxidant that calms irritated tissue and reduces swelling, making it an effective remedy for relieving redness and puffiness. Additionally, chamomile's natural antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles around the eyes, easing tension and discomfort. As a result, chamomile creams provide quick relief from sore, tired eyes, leaving them feeling refreshed and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and bisabolol, help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from irritation and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy vision.
Calming and soothing properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in chamomile have a sedative effect on the eyes, calming and soothing dry, itchy, or irritated eyes, and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers according to local regulations and guidelines for herbal use.
Combine 100 grams of dried chamomile flowers with 200 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow the herbs to infuse their medicinal properties into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler until the mixture reaches a smooth creamy consistency after 30 minutes of preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with sore eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated ocular tissue. The natural ingredients in elder cream, such as elderflower, chamomile, and calendula, work together to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of elder cream help to lock in moisture and protect the delicate skin around the eyes, promoting a healthy and comfortable visual experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elderberry contains anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin, which reduces swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from soreness and discomfort.

Antioxidant: Elderberry is rich in antioxidants like anthocyanins and flavonoids, which neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to eye tissues, helping to soothe and protect the eyes.

Antiviral: Elderberry has antiviral properties that help combat viral infections, such as conjunctivitis or blepharitis, that can cause sore eyes, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and flavonoids, elder roots are used to create anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of terpenes, elder rhyzomes have natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing eye redness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves, ensuring fresh and organic materials for the recipe.
Combine 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers with 40 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture over low flame, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and combined with the herbs.
Add 10 grams of coconut oil and 5 drops of vitamin e oil to the mixture, stirring well to incorporate.
Pour the cream into sterile glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime creams helps with sore eyes because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing instant relief from discomfort. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to soothe dryness and itchiness, leaving your eyes feeling calm and refreshed. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, you can experience rapid improvement in eye health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties: Tilia flowers contain flavonoids, terpenes, and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and combat infections in the eyes, making them useful for soothing sore eyes.

Antioxidant and free radical-scavenging properties: The antioxidants present in Tilia flowers can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye conditions like cataracts or age-related macular degeneration.

Calming and soothing effects: The flowers of the Lime tree have been traditionally used as a calming agent, which may help alleviate symptoms of irritated or sore eyes, such as redness, itchiness, and dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with sore eyes.
Barks: The barks contain tannins, which help to soothe and calm irritated eye tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves and flowers in the morning for maximum potency.
Dry the plant material using a dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours to preserve constituents.
Combine 20 grams of dried tilia platyphyllos with 40 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes under gentle agitation to facilitate melting and infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and let it cool before whipping into a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with sore eyes because of its unique combination of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. The extract from the maidenhair tree, specifically the leaves, contains high levels of beta-carotene and other carotenoids that help to soothe and calm irritated eyes. Additionally, the cream's rich moisturizing properties help to hydrate dry or inflamed eye tissues, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal Maidenhair Tree creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, soothing dryness, itchiness, and redness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The creams' anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with eye conditions like conjunctivitis, blepharitis, or dry eye syndrome.

Soothing and calming effects: The creams' gentle, non-irritating ingredients, including plant extracts like Adiantum capillus-veneris (Maidenhair Tree), have a calming effect on the eyes, providing relief from irritation, itching, and burning sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm irritated eye tissues.
Barks: Containing tannins that possess anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling associated with sore eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 20g of high-quality beeswax and add it to a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax until it reaches a temperature of 180°f then remove from heat.
Add 10ml of sweet almond oil 5g of ginkgo biloba extract and 2g of vitamin e oil to the melted beeswax.
Stir the mixture well for about 30 seconds to ensure all ingredients are fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool at room temperature for at least 60 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with sore eyes because they provide a soothing and calming effect on the delicate skin around the eyes. The natural anti-inflammatory properties of herbs such as chamomile, calendula, and licorice root work to reduce redness and swelling, while the rich, creamy texture gently cools and moisturizes the skin. As a result, herbal marshmallow creams can quickly alleviate dry, itchy, or irritated eyes, leaving them feeling comfortable and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Emollient properties: Marshmallow creams have emollient properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated or inflamed skin, including the delicate tissue around the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The presence of anti-inflammatory compounds in marshmallow cream can help reduce swelling and puffiness in the eyes, making them look more relaxed and comfortable.

Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow creams contain antioxidants that help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness, irritation, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which help soothe and lubricate the eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Buds: Containing antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, they aid in protecting the delicate eye tissues and promoting healthy healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality marshmallow root althaea officinalis in late summer.
Dry the harvested roots using a food dehydrator or air-drying method.
Grind 20 grams of dried marshmallow root into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 50 milliliters of coconut oil with 5 milliliters of beeswax and 10 grams of powdered marshmallow root in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for at least two hours before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams helps with sore eyes because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Horsetail is rich in silicon, which has been shown to soothe and calm irritated ocular tissues, reducing redness and itchiness. The cream's cooling and moisturizing effects also help to alleviate dryness and discomfort associated with tired or overworked eyes. Additionally, horsetail's antibacterial and antiviral properties can help to combat infections that may be contributing to eye irritation, providing long-lasting relief from sore, itchy, or tired eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Horsetail cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sore eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of horsetail cream help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye fatigue and dryness.

Soothing and calming properties: Horsetail's gentle, non-irritating texture and anti-inflammatory compounds provide a soothing and calming effect on irritated or inflamed eyes, promoting comfort and relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silicates, which help to soothe and calm irritated eye tissues.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and redness associated with sore eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean 100g of equisetum arvense plants ensuring no dirt or debris remains.
Combine the cleaned equisetum arvense with 500ml of distilled water in a stainless steel pot allowing it to steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids and retaining the liquid which now contains 25mg/ml of equisetum extract.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler heating it to 60°c while stirring until fully incorporated.
Combine the equisetum extract with the melted beeswax-coconut oil mixture and stir well adding 1 drop of lavender essential oil for a calming scent.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose creams helps with sore eyes because they contain soothing compounds that calm irritated tissues. The anti-inflammatory properties of beach rose extract reduce redness and swelling, while its antioxidant-rich formula neutralizes free radicals that can exacerbate dryness and discomfort. Additionally, the cream's emollient ingredients moisturize and nourish the delicate skin around the eyes, promoting healthy healing and relaxation. By providing instant relief from irritation and itchiness, beach rose creams offer a natural and effective solution for soothing sore eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Beach rose creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can cause soreness and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Beach rose extracts, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated or inflamed eyes.

Antimicrobial agents: Some beach rose creams may contain antimicrobial agents like salicylic acid, which can help prevent infections and reduce redness in sore eyes by eliminating bacteria and other microorganisms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling in sore eyes.
Barks: The barks are rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that help soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried rosa rugosa petals and calendula flowers in equal proportions weighing approximately 2 ounces each.
Combine the herbs with sweet almond oil in a double boiler heating the mixture at a low temperature for 4 hours.
Strain the oil infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Whip heavy cream and coconut oil together until smooth adding the infused oil mixture and mixing thoroughly.
Pour the final cream into sterilized glass jars sealing tightly and storing in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams help with sore eyes because of their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, including hyperforin and hypericin, work together to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by dryness, fatigue, or irritants. By soothing and calming the affected area, St John's Wort creams provide quick relief from discomfort and itchiness, promoting healthy vision and overall eye well-being. Additionally, the cream's gentle moisturizing properties help to hydrate and nourish the delicate skin around the eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: St. John's Wort contains anti-inflammatory compounds like hyperforin, which helps reduce swelling and inflammation in the eye, alleviating discomfort and pain.

Antioxidant properties: The herb has antioxidant properties, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of age-related macular degeneration and other eye problems.
Pain-relieving flavonoids: St. John's Wort creams contain flavonoids like hyperoside, which have been shown to have pain-relieving properties, helping to soothe irritated eyes and reduce discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain hyperforin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, making them effective in relieving eye redness and discomfort.

Flowers: The flowers of St John's Wort are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25g of dried hypericum perforatum flowers, 10g of beeswax pellets, and 50ml of sweet almond oil.
Combine 20g of dried hypericum perforatum flowers with 30ml of hot distilled water in a heatproof glass bowl.
Steep the mixture for 15 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the water and release their active compounds.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass measuring cup, discarding the solids.
Mix the cooled infusion with 20g of melted beeswax pellets and 30ml of sweet almond oil, whipping until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with sore eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, Melissa officinalis, contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in irritated eyes. When applied topically as a cream, lemon balm's soothing effects can quickly calm redness and itchiness, promoting relaxation and ease. Additionally, its antioxidant properties may help shield the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors, leaving them feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and swelling associated with eye strain or conjunctivitis.

Calming and sedative properties: The essential oils present in lemon balm cream, particularly citral and nerolidol, have a calming effect on the eyes, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-related eye tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Containing essential oils that provide anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in mid-summer for optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or dehydrator at low temperature for 24 hours.
Combine 30 grams of dried melissa officinalis with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat or in a double boiler until melted and well combined after 10-15 minutes.
Allow the cooled mixture to thicken for at least 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage creams helps with sore eyes because they contain the anti-inflammatory properties of borage oil, which soothes irritated eyes and reduces redness. The cream's rich moisturizing properties also help to hydrate dry, itchy eyes, while its antioxidant qualities combat free radicals that can cause eye strain and fatigue. Additionally, borage creams often contain soothing herbs like chamomile and calendula, which calm the eyes and promote a sense of relaxation, providing fast and effective relief from sore, irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): GLA, an omega-6 fatty acid found in borage oil, helps to reduce inflammation and soothe dryness in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of sore eyes such as redness and itchiness.

Vitamin A: Borage cream rich in vitamin A promotes healthy tear production, lubricating the eyes and reducing dryness, while also protecting against damage caused by free radicals that can contribute to sore eyes.

Antioxidants: The antioxidant properties of borage oil help to neutralize oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can be triggered by environmental factors such as UV light or air pollution, leading to sore and irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
Seeds: Containing gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an essential fatty acid that soothes and calms irritated eye tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis leaves and flowers to yield 20-25 grams of dried material.
Combine the dried plant material with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at a low temperature for 30 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse their properties into the oil and wax.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil-wax blend.
Allow the infused oil-wax blend to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30-60 minutes before transferring it to airtight containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wood sorrel creams helps with sore eyes because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eye tissues. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties, rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, work to calm redness and itching, while its astringent qualities help to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling. This natural combination provides instant relief for dry, tired, or irritated eyes, gently moisturizing and nourishing the delicate skin around the eye area, allowing you to enjoy clear and comfortable vision once again.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Wood sorrel cream's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate symptoms of sore and tired eyes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in wood sorrel cream help to reduce swelling and redness associated with sore eyes, providing relief from discomfort and irritation.

Soothing and calming effects: The natural soothing and calming properties of wood sorrel cream provide rapid relief from dry, itchy, or burning sensations in the eyes, promoting a sense of comfort and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, the barks have astringent properties that reduce swelling and alleviate redness in sore eyes.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh rumex acetosella leaves in abundance measure out one cup.
Chop the leaves finely to release their medicinal properties and essential oils.
Combine the chopped leaves with one tablespoon of olive oil and two tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler melt until the mixture reaches 180°f.
Remove the mixture from heat add one teaspoon of vitamin e oil and let it cool down completely.
Transfer the cooled mixture to an airtight container store in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with sore eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and triterpenoid acids, work together to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. Additionally, dandelion's soothing and moisturizing effects help to calm dry, irritated eyes and promote healthy tear production. This natural remedy provides relief from itchy, scratchy sensations and promotes overall eye comfort, making it a popular choice for those seeking a gentle and effective treatment for sore eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sore eyes.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties in dandelion cream help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye strain and fatigue.
Wound healing and soothing properties: Dandelion's wound-healing properties help soothe and calm irritated or inflamed tissue around the eyes, promoting faster recovery from eye irritations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and omega-3 oils, which help moisturize and protect the delicate tissues of the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh dandelion flowers and leaves and air-dry them for at least 2 weeks.
Combine the dried herbs with 50ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even infusion.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix 20ml of the infused oil with 10ml of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of distilled water, heating the mixture over low heat until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory creams helps with sore eyes because they provide instant relief to irritated and inflamed eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of chicory root extract in these creams reduce redness and swelling, while its natural soothing agents calm dryness and itchiness. As a natural remedy, chicory cream can be used repeatedly throughout the day without causing dependency or rebound effect. Its gentle, non-irritating formula makes it suitable for sensitive eyes and skin, providing long-lasting comfort and protection against sore and tired eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chicory cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes, which help reduce redness, swelling, and pain in sore eyes.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in chicory cream, such as flavonoids, neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, promoting healthy vision and soothing sore eyes.

Soothing demulcents: Chicory's mucilage (a thick, protective substance) forms a protective barrier on the surface of the eye, lubricating and calming irritated tissues, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chicory roots contain inulin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks of the chicory plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce eye inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cichorium intybus leaves and rinse them thoroughly under running water.
Combine 20 grams of dried cichorium intybus flowers with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 4 hours at a low temperature to extract the herbal active compounds.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids before adding 20 grams of beeswax pellets.
Heat the mixture gently while stirring until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the cream has thickened after 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with sore eyes because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, Glycyrrhiza glabra, contains glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with eye irritation. When applied topically, the cream calms the eyes and surrounding skin, providing instant relief from dryness, itchiness, and discomfort. Its antioxidant properties also help protect the eyes from further damage, promoting a healthy and comfortable vision experience.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Soothes and calms inflammation, reducing redness and discomfort associated with sore eyes.
Flavonoids: Antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy vision.
Saponins: Anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease pain in the eyes, providing quick relief from soreness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective for relieving eye irritation.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe sore eyes.
remedy_recipe: Extract glycyrrhizin from dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots using 100ml of hot water and 30 minutes of steeping time.
Mix the extracted liquid with 10g of beeswax, 20g of coconut oil, and 5g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and combined with the other ingredients.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 60°c before adding 10% vitamin e oil and 5% chamomile essential oil by volume.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized jars and let it solidify at room temperature for about 30 minutes before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for sore eyes are natural topical treatments made from plant extracts, essential oils, and other botanicals that provide relief from irritated, dry, or inflamed eyes. These creams help by reducing inflammation, soothing itchiness, and moisturizing the eyes to promote healing and comfort. Examples of herbal creams include those containing aloe vera, chamomile, and green tea, which have been shown to effectively alleviate symptoms of conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and dry eye syndrome. By using these creams, individuals can enjoy improved vision, reduced discomfort, and increased productivity in daily activities, allowing them to live life with renewed clarity and confidence.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat sore eyes with herbal creams is to apply a thin layer directly to the affected area. Choose creams containing soothing herbs like aloe vera, chamomile, or calendula, which have anti-inflammatory properties that calm redness and irritation. Gently massage the cream into your eyelids for 10-15 seconds before rinsing with warm water. Repeat as needed throughout the day for optimal relief.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sore eyes that are treated with herbal creams are conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and styes. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil can provide relief from inflammation, itchiness, and redness associated with these conditions. These natural remedies can also soothe dry eyes, reduce swelling, and promote healing.
